title: Fingerprint Pattern Classification Explorer
description: Identify the three fingerprint pattern families (loops, whorls, arches) and their eight subtypes by their defining structural features. Browse schematic ridge diagrams with annotated core, delta, and ridge-flow markers, then switch to Quiz Me mode to self-test classification with immediate feedback.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
Fingerprint Analysis
Dactyloscopy
Pattern Classification
Biometrics
bloomLevel: Remember (L1) — identify the fingerprint pattern families and subtypes by their defining features.

educational.topic: Classifying fingerprint patterns by core, delta count, and ridge flow
learningObjectives: Identify the three fingerprint pattern families: loops, whorls, and arches.
Name the eight subtypes and the structural feature that defines each.
Use delta count and ridge flow to classify a pattern: loops have one delta, whorls two, arches none.

pedagogical.interactionPattern: Annotated-diagram explorer with a self-test quiz. A left menu lists all eight patterns; selecting one shows an enlarged schematic ridge diagram with clickable Core / Delta / Ridge Flow annotations, a defining-features panel, and a family frequency badge. Quiz Me mode hides the annotations and asks the learner to classify an unknown print from a dropdown, then reveals the answer with the key distinguishing feature.
pedagogical.assessment: In Quiz Me mode the learner classifies a randomly chosen pattern from a dropdown; correct/incorrect feedback names the true pattern and its key feature, and the annotations are revealed
pedagogical.modelType: Schematic ridge renderer for eight pattern subtypes with parameterized core, delta, and ridge-flow annotation markers (not photographic prints)
controls: Click a pattern in the left menu to load it (study mode)
Click a Core / Delta / Ridge Flow chip for its definition
Quiz Me / Study Mode toggle button
Classification dropdown and Check Answer / Next Pattern button (quiz mode)
